name: Charleston

confRecord.note: 5-8-3 overall, 2-3-2 conf. Missed CAA Tourn. Hofstra won 4th consecutive CAA title.

notable: Battery connections provide occasional USL pathways
proPlayers.draftRank: Limited MLS pipeline; local USL Battery connections

acuAlignNote: Core kinesiology covered. Charleston's program is solid but smaller than major research universities. Good for a student who prioritises lifestyle and city over research facilities.

gpa.note: College of Charleston is accessible. GPA 2.8 is comfortable for both admission and aid.

fin.internationalNote: Affordable D1. Athletic scholarship can cover most costs. Academic merit available for 3.0+ GPA.

culture.vibe: One of America's most beautiful and charming cities. The College of Charleston sits in the heart of the historic district — cobblestone streets, antebellum architecture, and waterfront beauty. The campus atmosphere matches the city — relaxed, social, and genuinely enjoyable.
culture.thingsToDo: Rainbow Row architecture walks, Sullivan's Island and Isle of Palms beaches (20 min), Charleston Battery waterfront, King Street shopping and restaurants, Fort Sumter tours, Mount Pleasant Pier, Boone Hall Plantation. Charleston is endlessly photogenic.
culture.socialScene: Warm, Southern hospitality. Predominately domestic students but growing international presence. Charleston Battery soccer connection means players have local pro visibility. Beach access excellent. Social scene on Upper King Street is one of the best college bar strips in the South.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Charleston is a genuinely beautiful and liveable city. Warm, coastal, social — closer to Sydney lifestyle than most East Coast options. Smaller D1 program means personal attention. Excellent city culture.

facilityDetails.stadium: Patriots Point Soccer Complex — opened 2000, $6.5M facility, 2,000 capacity, natural grass, lights. Located 7 miles from main campus at Mt. Pleasant with stunning Charleston Harbour views from the pitch.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Training fields at Patriots Point and additional on-campus practice areas. Natural grass maintained to good standard. Year-round Charleston climate allows outdoor training 11+ months annually.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: CofC Athletics Weight Room — 8,000 sq ft, functional, adequate. Free weights, Olympic platforms, cable systems. Shared with all CofC varsity sports. Smaller program means more individual attention from S&C staff. Access to Charleston Battery training facilities occasionally available for top players.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) introduced 2023. Video analysis via Hudl. Kinesiology department has basic exercise physiology and biomechanics teaching labs. Less sophisticated sports science infrastructure than larger programs but growing.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CofC Athletics certified athletic trainer. Medical University of South Carolina (MUSC) sports medicine partnership — MUSC is a top academic medical centre with strong orthopaedics and sports cardiology. Good medical access for a mid-major D1 program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: CofC Department of Health and Human Performance: biomechanics teaching lab, exercise physiology lab, motor behaviour lab. MUSC clinical placement connections for pre-PT students are valuable — MUSC has a Doctor of Physical Therapy program on campus.
facilityDetails.extras: Patriots Point Naval Museum adjacent to soccer complex (USS Yorktown aircraft carrier). Charleston Harbour and beach access. Historic King Street and Market Street in downtown Charleston. Battery soccer facility has the best views of any soccer stadium in this guide — Charleston Harbour waterfront.
facilityDetails.note: Patriots Point is a gem — a beautiful facility in a stunning coastal location. But the overall sports science infrastructure is less developed than larger programs. The MUSC DPT program connection is a unique and under-appreciated advantage for a pre-PT student.

coach.name: Keith Wiggans
coach.title: Head Coach (6th season 2025)
coach.profile: 6th season as HC 2025. Overall record: 27-31-18, CAA: 10-19-8. 2024 best season: 10-6-2, ranked #23 nationally Sep 2024. CofC alum (2000-04) — holds school records for career GAA (0.99), wins (41), shutouts (23), saves (269). Played 7 seasons for Charleston Battery (USL). Was assistant/assoc HC under Ralph Lundy for 11 seasons before taking over 2019.

rec: Charleston is one of America's most beautiful cities — historic waterfront, warm climate. Keith Wiggans played for the local pro team (Charleston Battery). Best if Olivier values city lifestyle and a developing D1 program with personal coaching.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1 competitive: 4 rising juniors present. Yr2 Olivier proves himself while juniors become seniors. Yr3 major clearance opens leadership role.

staff.name: Jet Terry
staff.bg: Hired March 2025. Charleston native, former CofC player 2016. 2 seasons as assistant at Saginaw Valley State (D2) where he helped win conf title and reach NCAA QF. Previously Director of Soccer Operations at CofC 2022-23.

staff.name: Ernesto Aguinaga-Hernandez
staff.bg: Joined July 2025. Previously volunteer assistant at Univ of Wisconsin-Parkside 4 seasons. Helped 2 conference titles, 3 NCAA D2 tournaments. Former trialist Club Leon FC (Mexico).

name: William & Mary
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology and health sciences pathway. Pre-PT advising available. Williamsburg is near Sentara Healthcare for clinical access.

gpa.note: Selective public liberal arts college. Strong academic profile required.

confRecord.note: CAA regular season. Did not qualify for tournament.

coach.name: Shaun Tsakiris
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Long-tenured W&M coach. Consistently strong CAA program.

rec: Best academic profile in the CAA. Small colonial campus. Consistently competitive program — good for development path.

name: Hofstra
acuAlignNote: Health Sciences covers anatomy, physiology. NYC proximity (30 min via LIRR) opens extensive clinical placement for pre-PT.

gpa.note: Private university with flexible admission. Athletic scholarships standard.

confRecord.note: 4th consecutive CAA Tournament title. 9th NCAA appearance. Beat UNCW in final.
3rd consecutive CAA Tournament title. No. 7 NCAA seed.

coach.name: Scott Coufal
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Building Hofstra in CAA. Long Island/NYC access is a unique recruiting asset.

rec: NYC proximity via LIRR is the main appeal. Solid CAA program. Expensive for the tier.

name: Northeastern
acuAlignNote: Strong health sciences and neuroscience pathways. Co-op program provides real clinical placement in Boston hospital system — excellent for pre-PT.

gpa.note: Selective private university with strong co-op placement. Athletic scholarship available.

confRecord.note: CAA conference play. Beat Hofstra 3-1 in opener.

coach.name: Brian Ainscough
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Building Northeastern in CAA. Boston location and co-op model are program differentiators.

rec: Boston city + co-op clinical placement in hospitals = outstanding pre-PT pathway. Expensive but co-op offsets some cost. Soccer is mid-CAA.

name: Drexel
acuAlignNote: Health Sciences covers anatomy, physiology. Co-op program provides clinical placements in Philadelphia hospital network for pre-PT.

gpa.note: Selective private university. Co-op model reduces overall cost over 5-year program.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Philadelphia-based program with co-op clinical placement advantages.

rec: Philadelphia city + co-op clinical placements. Similar to Northeastern but in Philly. Soccer is lower-tier CAA.

name: Delaware
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology and applied physiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science well-covered. Pre-PT pathway supported.

gpa.note: Public university with strong academics. Athletic scholarship pathway established.

confRecord.note: Delaware left CAA for CUSA July 2025.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Strong CAA program. Applied Physiology degree is an asset.

rec: Strong kinesiology degree. Consistent CAA soccer. Low cost. Newark is a smaller college town — limited lifestyle.

name: Elon
acuAlignNote: Exercise science covers core subjects. Pre-PT advising available. Small beautiful campus.

gpa.note: Private university with merit+athletic scholarship opportunities.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Small private university with strong campus culture.

rec: Beautiful small campus. Rural North Carolina — low lifestyle fit. Mild climate is a marginal plus.

name: Monmouth
acuAlignNote: Health studies covers core anatomy and physiology. NJ Shore location with NYC access for clinical placements.

gpa.note: Accessible private university. Athletic scholarship packages available.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing CAA program on the New Jersey shore.

rec: NJ Shore location has appeal — beach access, NYC day trips. Soccer and academics are developing tier. Best for maximum minutes opportunity.

name: Stony Brook
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Stony Brook Medicine is a top research medical centre — strong pre-PT access.

gpa.note: Public research university (SUNY). Strong academic profile for the cost.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing Stony Brook in CAA. Strong research university with good pre-PT access.

rec: Strong value — low cost, research university, Stony Brook Medicine for pre-PT. Rural Long Island campus. NYC access via LIRR.

name: Towson
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Baltimore and DC proximity provides strong clinical placement for pre-PT.

gpa.note: Accessible public university. Strong scholarship packages for international athletes.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: CAA program in Baltimore suburb. DC/Baltimore medical district access.

rec: Best value in the CAA. Baltimore suburb + DC day trips. Low cost. Soccer is developing but pre-PT pathway via local hospitals is good.
